title: product author domain — MCP tool surface
description: The complete tool contract for the What-capture session. Each tool constructs a valid graph fragment against the Product Framework ontology and validates it in-loop against shapes.shacl.ttl. Every mutating tool returns { ok, node, violations[] }; a violation carries the framework-section message so the model self-corrects. Tools are structured operations — the model never emits raw Turtle.
produces: A conformant domain+event graph (Product Framework Level 1: Described).

id.description: Stable node identifier, unique within the product graph.

label.description: Human-readable name, in the ubiquitous language of its context.

result.description: The standard return of every mutating tool.

ok.description: True if the fragment was accepted (no blocking violations).

node.description: The id of the created/updated node.

violations.description: SHACL violations, each with the framework-section message. Non-empty + ok:false means rejected; the model must correct and retry.

message.description: e.g. '§3.2 every event must change a real entity'.

tools.name: session_start
tools.description: Open or create the domain graph for a product. Optionally seed from a prior session's graph. Call once at the start.

product.description: The product whose What is being captured.

seed_graph.description: Optional Turtle to seed from (e.g. a partial model from a prior session).

participants.description: Names/roles in the room (recorded in provenance).

tools.name: session_state
tools.description: Return a summary of the current graph (counts per class), the conformance status, and the current open questions. Read-only.

tools.name: session_finalize
tools.description: Run full SHACL validation; if conformant, export the graph as Turtle and return a provenance record (hash, participants, timestamp, tool-call count). If non-conformant, return the blocking violations and do not finalize.

tools.name: add_bounded_context
tools.description: Create a bounded context: a region within which every term has exactly one meaning (§3.1). The domain model is a set of these — never one flat model.

purpose.description: What this context is responsible for.

glossary.description: Authoritative ubiquitous-language terms for this context.

tools.name: add_entity
tools.description: Create an entity: a domain concept with identity, placed in exactly one bounded context (§3.1). May be an aggregate root.

context.description: The bounded context this entity belongs to (must exist).

identity.description: What makes one instance distinct (e.g. 'email is unique').

tools.name: add_value_object
tools.description: Create a value object: a domain concept without identity (e.g. Money, Address), in a bounded context.

tools.name: add_relation
tools.description: Create a typed relation between two entities. Cardinality AND rationale are both required (§3.1) — the rationale is why the link and the cardinality are what they are.

rationale.description: Why this link and this cardinality (e.g. 'we bill the company, not the person').

tools.name: add_invariant
tools.description: Create an invariant: a rule that must always hold, stated as a checkable constraint (§3.1). Becomes a SHACL constraint downstream.

statement.description: e.g. 'a Subscription cannot be active without a PaymentMethod'.

tools.name: add_context_mapping
tools.description: Declare an explicit correspondence between concepts in two different bounded contexts (§3.1) — the resolver for 'is a User a Customer?'. Never assume; always declare.

concept_b.description: The corresponding concept in another context.

kind.description: The DDD relationship type, if known.

rationale.description: The correspondence (e.g. 'same person, linked by PersonId').

tools.name: add_command
tools.description: Create a command: an intent that causes events (§3.2). Targets an aggregate (entity) and emits at least one event.

targets.description: The aggregate (entity) this command operates on (must exist).

emits.description: The event(s) this command produces (must exist or be created alongside).

tools.name: add_event
tools.description: Create an event: a past-tense, immutable fact (§3.2). MUST change a real domain entity — behaviour may not reference structure that does not exist (the load-bearing relation).

changes.description: The entity whose state this event changes (must exist). Enforced by §3.2.

tools.name: add_read_model
tools.description: Create a read model: a view built to look at or decide on (§3.2). Projects at least one entity/event — no ghost views (read-model provenance).

projects.description: The entities/events this view is built from (must exist).

tools.name: add_wireframe_step
tools.description: Create a UI step in a flow: what the user sees, which triggers a command or displays a read model. Uses ubiquitous-language terms.

displays.description: The read model this step shows (optional).

tools.name: add_flow
tools.description: Assemble an ordered behaviour: a timeline of wireframe → command → event(s) → read model (§3.2). Model the interesting (core) flows only; not trivial CRUD.

steps.description: Ordered references to wireframe steps, commands, events, and read models.

tools.name: open_questions
tools.description: The facilitation driver. Return the current SHACL gaps as questions for the room — e.g. an entity with no context, an event that changes nothing, a relation with no rationale, a command with no events. Read-only.

focus.description: Limit the questions to the domain (structure) or event (behaviour) half.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Read the graph. Either a convenience query ('what happens to X', 'contents of context C', 'relations of entity E') or a raw SPARQL SELECT. Read-only.

about.description: A node to describe (its links in and out).

tools.name: validate
tools.description: Run all SHACL shapes over the whole graph and return conformance plus any violations (each with its framework-section message). Read-only; also run automatically inside every mutating tool for the affected node.
